events.note: Writing Intent Check (revision 1) for the ECMP false-positive task

events.change_summary: Intent Check (revision 1): problem framing, expected outcomes, and scope boundaries for cutting ECMP-driven High Jitter false positives

events.summary: Intent Check pushed as revision 1. Covers all three levers from the brief: larger probe samples in the fallback, a Paris-capable prober when available, and preferring nearby Globalping measurements for the loss/jitter verdict. Approve to proceed to the full scope, or reply with refinements.

events.note: Writing full scope (revision 2) after approved intent check

events.change_summary: Full scope: 11 requirements across fallback sampling, Paris-capable probing, and near-target verdict gating; 8 acceptance criteria; 3 implementation phases with files and verification

events.rationale: Fixes existing broken behavior (false High Jitter verdicts) with no UI beyond existing terminal tables — skip design. Work follows established single-purpose module conventions (new paris.py mirrors pmtu.py/latency.py, diagnosis gating mirrors the rate-limited-hop pattern) and touches ~6 files across trace, diagnosis, and remote-measurement layers without new system boundaries — skip architecture; detailed phases with Files and Verification carry the design.

events.decision: All three levers from the brief are in scope as separate phases: raise the fallback sample count, add best-effort Paris-capable prober support, and gate country-sweep verdicts on Globalping near-target loss/jitter.
events.rationale: The levers address independent failure modes — sample-size quantization, ECMP flow divergence, and vantage-point bias — and each is independently shippable. Paris probers need the same raw-socket privileges that already force mtr into the fallback on unprivileged macOS, so phase 2 is strictly best-effort (silent fall-through to system traceroute) and phases 1 and 3 must eliminate the false positives on their own.

events.decision: Remote-figure verdict gating lives inside the pure diagnose() function via new keys on the result dict (ping_loss_pct, ping_jitter_ms in the existing per-row globalping dict); country mode re-runs diagnose() on rows that gained remote data after the Globalping merge, before the summary table and exit-code computation. The jitter threshold (JITTER_WARNING_MS = 10.0) is unchanged.
events.rationale: Follows the established project rule that context-dependent signal re-classification belongs in diagnosis.py (the pure-function module), not in transport modules or cli.py. Re-diagnosis is required because Globalping results merge after diagnose() already ran inside _measure(); without it the summary verdicts and exit code would ignore the near-target data. Keeping the threshold fixed keeps the change about input quality, not calibration drift.

events.summary: Full scope pushed as revision 2: 11 requirements, 8 acceptance criteria, 3 phases. Phase 1 fixes the fallback prober to honor the requested probe count (the probes parameter is currently ignored and -q 2 is hardcoded) and gates the High Jitter check on sample size. Phase 2 adds a best-effort paris.py module (dublin-traceroute, then scamper) into the fallback chain with silent fall-through. Phase 3 raises Globalping ping to 16 packets, parses near-target loss/jitter, and re-derives country-sweep verdicts after the remote merge so the summary and exit code reflect near-target quality. Workflow: bug-fix / standard, no design or architecture stage.

workflow.rationale: Fixes existing broken behavior (false High Jitter verdicts) with no UI beyond existing terminal tables — skip design. Work follows established single-purpose module conventions (new paris.py mirrors pmtu.py/latency.py, diagnosis gating mirrors the rate-limited-hop pattern) and touches ~6 files across trace, diagnosis, and remote-measurement layers without new system boundaries — skip architecture; detailed phases with Files and Verification carry the design.
